clinical_history: ECG from an elderly adult man. What does the rhythm strip show? (Note: baseline artifact is present possibly due to movement.)
diagnosis: The ECG shows underlying sinus rhythm with PACs. There is a normal QRS axis and normal intervals. Of note, evidence of left atrial abnormality is present. (Biphasic P wave in lead V1 with broad, terminal negative component and broad notched P waves in other leads.) There is also nonspecific slow precordial r wave progression (V1-V4) and non-diagnostic inferior Q waves. The patient had a history of atrial flutter/fibrillation in the context of advanced age, systemic hypertension, and type 2 diabetes mellitus.  Echocardiographic imaging had shown left atrial dilation with preserved left ventricular systolic function. The P wave features are best seen in magnified views.
